title: Federal Reserve Board releases annual audited financial statements
summary: The Federal Reserve Board released the annual audited financial statements for the Federal Reserve System for 2025, covering the 12 Federal Reserve Banks, the Board, and one limited liability company related to COVID-19 pandemic response. An independent accounting firm issued unqualified opinions, finding no material misstatements in accordance with applicable auditing standards.
key_points: Annual audited financial statements cover the Federal Reserve System as of December 31, 2025
Independent accounting firm issued unqualified opinions, finding no material misstatements
Audits included internal controls over financial reporting for the 12 Reserve Banks and the Board
The Federal Reserve Act requires annual independent audits of each Reserve Bank and the Board
The Board also publishes weekly comprehensive balance sheet and quarterly financial reports
One LLC included in the statements relates to the Board's COVID-19 pandemic response
why_it_matters: This is a routine compliance disclosure required by the Federal Reserve Act. It provides transparency into the Federal Reserve's financial reporting and audit processes but does not convey any monetary policy signals, rate guidance, economic projections, or market-sensitive information. The audited statements cover operations as of December 31, 2025, and include internal controls audits for the 12 Reserve Banks and the Board. This is not a macro catalyst.
